audit_items.proof_trace: GetPixelWriteMask() returns a Quantum value (a scalar arithmetic type — unsigned char/short/int or float depending on build configuration), not a pointer; it cannot return NULL. All four call sites in compare.c (lines 431, 1920, 2059, 2277) correctly compare the scalar return against QuantumRange/2 with no pointer dereference involved. The property's premise is incorrect: no NULL-check is needed or applicable.
